title: Symbols, predicates and rules deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap for Symbols, predicates and rules; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Symbols, predicates and rules: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? If a fact is not stored in a symbolic knowledge base, it is false in every reasoning system.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Declare the vocabulary, domain, identity, and open- or closed-world policy; distinguish explicit negation, contradiction, and missing knowledge.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Declare the vocabulary, domain, identity, and open- or closed-world policy; distinguish explicit negation, contradiction, and missing knowledge.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Declare the vocabulary, domain, identity, and open- or closed-world policy; distinguish explicit negation, contradiction, and missing knowledge.
If a fact is not stored in a symbolic knowledge base, it is false in every reasoning system.

answer.explanation: Declare the vocabulary, domain, identity, and open- or closed-world policy; distinguish explicit negation, contradiction, and missing knowledge.

items.title: Symbols, predicates and rules: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'missing knowledge'. Declare the vocabulary, domain, identity, and open- or closed-world policy; distinguish explicit negation, contradiction, and missing knowledge.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Declare the vocabulary, domain, identity, and open- or closed-world policy; distinguish explicit negation, contradiction, and missing knowledge.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Declare the vocabulary, domain, identity, and open- or closed-world policy; distinguish explicit negation, contradiction, and [[checkpoint]].

answer.explanation: Declare the vocabulary, domain, identity, and open- or closed-world policy; distinguish explicit negation, contradiction, and missing knowledge.
